plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce overall risk.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% and QQQ to 20% to favor income and broad index exposure.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single name concentration.
This plan aims for capital preservation and lower volatility.
Maintain a significant cash buffer by reducing overall market exposure.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a modest ~1.5%/month, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower drawdown. Expect reduced upside potential in strong bull markets.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with concentration limits.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to eliminate holdings below stop and reduce leverage.
Increase QQQI to 22% for a balanced income component.
Buy AMD at 8% of the book, leveraging a high-scoring long setup.
Trim CRWD to 12% to manage single name concentration.
This plan balances risk and growth, maintaining a moderate cash buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while introducing a high-quality new long. Expect moderate drawdowns and growth potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits while maintaining broad exposure.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit positions below stop and reallocate capital.
Increase CRWD to 18% to press a strong performing position.
Buy AMD at 10% and SNOW at 7%, focusing on high-scoring long setups.
This plan leans into growth and higher conviction plays.
Maintain strict stop discipline given the increased leverage and concentration.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month, aiming for stretched growth. It utilizes conviction in strong performers and new high-scoring setups, which amplifies both upside and potential drawdown. Leverage decay in ETFs and rapid losses are significant risks if stops are not strictly adhered to.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and free up capital.
Sell all SOXL, SPAL, CRWD, and SMH to eliminate leveraged ETFs and high-beta names.
Increase QQQI to 40% to maximize income generation.
Trim QQQ to 15% to further reduce growth-oriented exposure.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown.
Maintain a cash buffer for stability and rebalancing.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month, focusing on reliable distributions and capital preservation. It significantly reduces growth exposure and volatility, aiming for cash-like income. Distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in market downturns.
